content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/janfr/.claude/skills/gsd-autonomous

<objective>
Execute all remaining milestone phases autonomously. For each phase: discuss → plan → execute. Pauses only for user decisions (grey area acceptance, blockers, validation requests).

Uses ROADMAP.md phase discovery and Skill() flat invocations for each phase command. After all phases complete: milestone audit → complete → cleanup.

**Creates/Updates:**
- `.planning/STATE.md` — updated after each phase
- `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — progress updated after each phase
- Phase artifacts — CONTEXT.md, PLANs, SUMMARYs per phase

**After:** Milestone is complete and cleaned up.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@$HOME/.claude/gsd-core/workflows/autonomous.md
@$HOME/.claude/gsd-core/references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Optional flags:
- `--from N` — start from phase N instead of the first incomplete phase.
- `--to N` — stop after phase N completes (halt instead of advancing to next phase).
- `--only N` — execute only phase N (single-phase mode).
- `--interactive` — run discuss inline with questions (not auto-answered), then dispatch plan→execute as background agents. Keeps the main context lean while preserving user input on decisions.
- `--converge` — run each phase's planning step through `gsd-plan-review-convergence` instead of plain `gsd-plan-phase`. Requires `workflow.plan_review_convergence=true`.
- `--cross-ai` — compatibility alias for `--converge`.

When `--converge` or `--cross-ai` is set, reviewer selector flags supported by `gsd-plan-review-convergence` may be passed through: `--codex`, `--gemini`, `--claude`, `--opencode`, `--ollama`, `--lm-studio`, `--llama-cpp`, `--all`, and `--max-cycles N`.

Project context, phase list, and state are resolved inside the workflow using init commands (`gsd-tools query init.milestone-op`, `gsd-tools query roadmap.analyze`). No upfront context loading needed.
</context>

<process>
Execute end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (phase discovery, per-phase execution, blocker handling, progress display).
</process>


ARGUMENTS: Do phase 2. Start by doing "/gsd-review --phase 2" with codex. Then continue with the GSD commands to integrate the feedback from codex and then to implement and review the code until phase 2 totally done
I'll start by loading the GSD autonomous workflow skill.

input.args: Do phase 2. Start by doing "/gsd-review --phase 2" with codex. Then continue with the GSD commands to integrate the feedback from codex and then to implement and review the code until phase 2 totally done

result.output: Launching skill: gsd-autonomous

content.text: Skill /gsd-autonomous is already loaded above; instructions unchanged. Arguments: Do phase 2. Start by doing "/gsd-review --phase 2" with codex. Then continue with the GSD commands to integrate the feedback from codex and then to implement and review the code until phase 2 totally done

result.output:
1	<purpose>
2	
3	Drive milestone phases autonomously — all remaining phases, a range via `--from N`/`--to N`, or a single phase via `--only N`. For each incomplete phase: discuss → plan → execute using Skill() flat invocations. When `--converge` or `--cross-ai` is set, route the planning step through plan-review convergence before execution. Pauses only for explicit user decisions (grey area acceptance, blockers, validation requests). Re-reads ROADMAP.md after each phase to catch dynamically inserted phases.
4	
5	</purpose>
6	
7	<required_reading>
8	
9	Read all files referenced by the invoking prompt's execution_context before starting.
10	
11	</required_reading>
12	
13	<process>
14	
15	<step name="initialize" priority="first">
16	
17	## 1. Initialize
18	
19	Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for `--from N`, `--to N`, `--only N`, `--interactive`, `--converge`/`--cross-ai`, reviewer selector flags, and `--max-cycles N`:
20	
21	```bash
22	FROM_PHASE=""
23	if echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -qE '\-\-from\s+[0-9]'; then
24	  FROM_PHASE=$(echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -oE '\-\-from\s+[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*' | awk '{print $2}')
25	fi
26	
27	TO_PHASE=""
28	if echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -qE '\-\-to\s+[0-9]'; then
29	  TO_PHASE=$(echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -oE '\-\-to\s+[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*' | awk '{print $2}')
30	fi
31	
32	ONLY_PHASE=""
33	if echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -qE '\-\-only\s+[0-9]'; then
34	  ONLY_PHASE=$(echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -oE '\-\-only\s+[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*' | awk '{print $2}')
35	  FROM_PHASE="$ONLY_PHASE"
36	fi
37	
38	INTERACTIVE=""
39	if echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -q '\-\-interactive'; then
40	  INTERACTIVE="true"
41	fi
42	
43	PLAN_STRATEGY="local"
44	if echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -qE '(^|[[:space:]])\-\-(converge|cross-ai)([[:space:]]|$)'; then
45	  PLAN_STRATEGY="converge"
46	fi
47	
48	CONVERGENCE_ARGS=""
49	for REVIEW_FLAG in --codex --gemini --claude --opencode --ollama --lm-studio --llama-cpp --all --text; do
50	  if echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]])${REVIEW_FLAG}([[:space:]]|$)"; then
51	    CONVERGENCE_ARGS="${CONVERGENCE_ARGS} ${REVIEW_FLAG}"
52	  fi
53	done
54	
55	MAX_CYCLES_ARG=""
56	if echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -qE '\-\-max-cycles\s+[0-9]+'; then
57	  MAX_CYCLES_ARG=$(echo "$ARGUMENTS" | grep -oE '\-\-max-cycles\s+[0-9]+' | awk '{print $2}')
58	  CONVERGENCE_ARGS="${CONVERGENCE_ARGS} --max-cycles ${MAX_CYCLES_ARG}"
59	fi
60	```
61	
62	When `--only` is set, also set `FROM_PHASE` to the same value so existing filter logic applies.
63	
64	When `--interactive` is set, discuss stays inline. If `dispatch-should-flatten` returns `false`, dispatch plan and execute as background agents; if it returns `true`, run them inline and keep phases sequential. Preserve user input on all design decisions.
65	
66	When `PLAN_STRATEGY=converge`, the planning step MUST invoke the plan-review convergence workflow instead of `gsd-plan-phase`. `--cross-ai` is an alias for `--converge`. Forward `CONVERGENCE_ARGS` exactly as parsed so reviewer flags and `--max-cycles N` retain the same meaning as they have on `/gsd-plan-review-convergence`.
67	
68	Bootstrap via milestone-level init:
69	
70	```bash
71	_GSD_SHIM_NAME="gsd-tools.cjs"; _GSD_RUNTIME_ROOT="${RUNTIME_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)}"; GSD_TOOLS="${_GSD_RUNTIME_ROOT}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; if [ -f "$GSD_TOOLS" ]; then gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${_GSD_RUNTIME_ROOT}/.claude/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${_GSD_RUNTIME_ROOT}/.claude/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${_GSD_RUNTIME_ROOT}/.codex/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${_GSD_RUNTIME_ROOT}/.codex/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif command -v gsd-tools >/dev/null 2>&1; then GSD_TOOLS="$(command -v gsd-tools)"; gsd_run() { "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${HERMES_HOME:-$HOME/.hermes}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${CURSOR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.cursor}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${CURSOR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.cursor}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${GEMINI_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.gemini}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${GEMINI_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.gemini}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${COPILOT_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.copilot}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${COPILOT_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.copilot}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${WINDSURF_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.codeium/windsurf}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${WINDSURF_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.codeium/windsurf}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${AUGMENT_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.augment}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${AUGMENT_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.augment}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${TRAE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.trae}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${TRAE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.trae}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${QWEN_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.qwen}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${QWEN_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.qwen}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${CODEBUDDY_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.codebuddy}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${CODEBUDDY_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.codebuddy}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${CLINE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.cline}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${CLINE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.cline}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${GROK_AGENTS_HOME:-$HOME/.agents}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${GROK_AGENTS_HOME:-$HOME/.agents}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${ANTIGRAVITY_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.gemini/antigravity}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${ANTIGRAVITY_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.gemini/antigravity}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR:-${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/opencode}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR:-${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/opencode}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${KILO_CONFIG_DIR:-${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/kilo}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${KILO_CONFIG_DIR:-${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/kilo}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; else echo "ERROR: gsd-tools.cjs not found at $GSD_TOOLS and gsd-tools is not on PATH. Run: npx -y @opengsd/gsd-core@latest --claude --local" >&2; exit 1; fi; if [ -n "${CLAUDE_ENV_FILE:-}" ] && [ -n "${GSD_TOOLS:-}" ]; then printf "export PATH='%s':\"\$PATH\"\n" "${GSD_TOOLS%/*}" >> "$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true; fi
72	INIT=$(gsd_run query init.milestone-op)
73	if [[ "$INIT" == @file:* ]]; then INIT=$(cat "${INIT#@file:}"); fi
74	```
75	
76	If `PLAN_STRATEGY` is `converge`, fail fast unless the existing convergence feature gate is enabled:
77	
78	```bash
79	if [ "$PLAN_STRATEGY" = "converge" ]; then
80	  CONVERGENCE_ENABLED=$(gsd_run query config-get workflow.plan_review_convergence 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
81	  if [ "$CONVERGENCE_ENABLED" != "true" ]; then
82	    printf '%s\n' \
83	      'gsd-autonomous --converge is disabled (workflow.plan_review_convergence=false).' \
84	      '' \
85	      'Enable plan convergence with:' \
86	      '' \
87	      '  gsd config-set workflow.plan_review_convergence true' \
88	      '' \
89	      'Then re-run the autonomous command with --converge.'
90	    exit 1
91	  fi
92	fi
93	```
94	
95	Parse JSON for: `milestone_version`, `milestone_name`, `phase_count`, `completed_phases`, `roadmap_exists`, `state_exists`, `commit_docs`.
96	
97	**If `roadmap_exists` is false:** Error — "No ROADMAP.md found. Run `/gsd-new-milestone` first."
98	**If `state_exists` is false:** Error — "No STATE.md found. Run `/gsd-new-milestone` first."
99	
100	Display startup banner:
101	
102	```
103	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
104	 GSD ► AUTONOMOUS
105	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
106	
107	 Milestone: {milestone_version} — {milestone_name}
108	 Phases: {phase_count} total, {completed_phases} complete
109	```
110	
111	If `ONLY_PHASE` is set, display: `Single phase mode: Phase ${ONLY_PHASE}`
112	Else if `FROM_PHASE` is set, display: `Starting from phase ${FROM_PHASE}`
113	If `TO_PHASE` is set, display: `Stopping after phase ${TO_PHASE}`
114	If `INTERACTIVE` is set, display: `Mode: Interactive (discuss inline, plan+execute inline — background on Codex only)`
115	If `PLAN_STRATEGY` is `converge`, display: `Planning: Plan-review convergence enabled`
116	
117	**Agent skills (delegated agents self-load):** This workflow delegates plan/execute/review via flat `Skill()` invocations rather than resolving `agent_skills` itself. Each consumer agent (`gsd-planner`, `gsd-executor`, `gsd-plan-checker`, `gsd-verifier`, …) self-loads its configured `.planning/config.json` `agent_skills` in its own mandatory init step per `@$HOME/.claude/gsd-core/references/agent-skills-bootstrap.md`. This is the durable path that works on every runtime — including Cursor, where `Skill()`-delegated workflow bash init does not reliably execute. No per-delegation injection is needed here. See open-gsd/gsd-core#1866.
118	
119	</step>
120	
121	<step name="discover_phases">
122	
123	## 2. Discover Phases
124	
125	Run phase discovery:
126	
127	```bash
128	INIT_MANAGER=$(gsd_run query init.manager)
129	if [[ "$INIT_MANAGER" == @file:* ]]; then INIT_MANAGER=$(cat "${INIT_MANAGER#@file:}"); fi
130	STATE_CONTENT=$(cat .planning/STATE.md 2>/dev/null || true)
131	```
132	
133	Parse the JSON `phases` array.
134	
135	Parse the optional `## Deferred Verification` table from `STATE_CONTENT` into a phase-number map:
136	- `verification_deferred_human` -> `/gsd-verify-work <phase>`
137	- `verification_deferred_gaps` -> `/gsd-plan-phase <phase> --gaps`
138	
139	**Skip deferred phases on autonomous re-entry:** drop any phase whose number appears in the deferred-phase map from this run's queue; resume it only through the recorded command.
140	
141	**Filter to incomplete phases:** Keep `phase_complete !== true`, including implemented phases with `verification_status !== "passed"`.
142	
143	**Apply `--from N`:** If set, filter out phases where `number < FROM_PHASE` (numeric compare; handles "5.1").
144	
145	**Apply `--to N`:** If set, filter out phases where `number > TO_PHASE` (numeric compare).
146	
147	**Apply `--only N`:** If set, filter out phases where `number != ONLY_PHASE`.
148	
149	**If `TO_PHASE` is set and no phases remain** (all phases up to N are already completed):
150	
151	```
152	All phases through ${TO_PHASE} are already completed. Nothing to do.
153	```
154	
155	Exit cleanly.
156	
157	**If `ONLY_PHASE` is set and no phases remain** (phase already complete):
158	
159	```
160	Phase ${ONLY_PHASE} is already complete. Nothing to do.
161	```
162	
163	Exit cleanly.
164	
165	**Sort by `number`** in numeric ascending order.
166	
167	**If no incomplete phases remain:**
168	
169	```
170	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
171	 GSD ► AUTONOMOUS ▸ COMPLETE 🎉
172	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
173	
174	 All phases complete! Nothing left to do.
175	```
176	
177	Exit cleanly.
178	
179	**Display phase plan:**
180	
181	```
182	## Phase Plan
183	
184	| # | Phase | Status |
185	|---|-------|--------|
186	| 5 | Skill Scaffolding & Phase Discovery | In Progress |
187	| 6 | Smart Discuss | Not Started |
188	| 7 | Auto-Chain Refinements | Not Started |
189	| 8 | Lifecycle Orchestration | Not Started |
190	```
191	
192	**If any deferred phases were skipped:** display `## Deferred Verification (Skipped on Re-entry)` with the skipped rows and resume commands, then omit them from this run's queue.
193	
194	**Fetch details for each phase:**
195	
196	```bash
197	DETAIL=$(gsd_run query roadmap.get-phase ${PHASE_NUM})
198	```
199	
200	Extract `phase_name`, `goal`, `success_criteria` from each. Store for use in execute_phase and transition messages.
201	
202	</step>
203	
204	<step name="execute_phase">
205	
206	## 3. Execute Phase
207	
208	For the current phase, display the progress banner:
209	
210	```
211	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
212	 GSD ► AUTONOMOUS ▸ Phase {N}/{T}: {Name} [████░░░░] {P}%
213	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
214	```
215	
216	Where N is the ROADMAP phase number, T is the milestone `phase_count`, and P = completed milestone phases / T × 100. Use `phase_count`, not remaining phases: phase 63 in a 7-phase milestone is `Phase 63/7`, not `Phase 63/3`. If N > T, render `Phase {N} ({position}/{T})`. Use an 8-character bar with █ and ░.
217	
218	**3a. Smart Discuss**
219	
220	Check if CONTEXT.md already exists for this phase:
221	
222	```bash
223	PHASE_STATE=$(gsd_run query init.phase-op ${PHASE_NUM})
224	```
225	
226	Parse `has_context` from JSON.
227	
228	**If has_context is true:** Skip discuss — context already gathered. Display:
229	
230	```
231	Phase ${PHASE_NUM}: Context exists — skipping discuss.
232	```
233	
234	Proceed to 3b.
235	
236	**If has_context is false:** Check if discuss is disabled via settings:
237	
238	```bash
239	SKIP_DISCUSS=$(gsd_run query config-get workflow.skip_discuss 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
240	```
241	
242	**If SKIP_DISCUSS is `true`:** Skip discuss entirely — the ROADMAP phase description is the spec. Display:
243	
244	```
245	Phase ${PHASE_NUM}: Discuss skipped (workflow.skip_discuss=true) — using ROADMAP phase goal as spec.
246	```
247	
248	Write a minimal CONTEXT.md so downstream plan-phase has valid input. Get phase details:
249	
250	```bash
251	DETAIL=$(gsd_run query roadmap.get-phase ${PHASE_NUM})
252	```
253	
254	Extract `goal` and `requirements` from JSON. Write `${phase_dir}/${padded_phase}-CONTEXT.md` with:
255	
256	```markdown
257	# Phase {PHASE_NUM}: {Phase Name} - Context
258	
259	**Gathered:** {date}
260	**Status:** Ready for planning
261	**Mode:** Auto-generated (discuss skipped via workflow.skip_discuss)
262	
263	<domain>
264	## Phase Boundary
265	
266	{goal from ROADMAP phase description}
267	
268	</domain>
269	
270	<decisions>
271	## Implementation Decisions
272	
273	### Claude's Discretion
274	All implementation choices are at Claude's discretion — discuss phase was skipped per user setting. Use ROADMAP phase goal, success criteria, and codebase conventions to guide decisions.
275	
276	</decisions>
277	
278	<code_context>
279	## Existing Code Insights
280	
281	Codebase context will be gathered during plan-phase research.
282	
283	</code_context>
284	
285	<specifics>
286	## Specific Ideas
287	
288	No specific requirements — discuss phase skipped. Refer to ROADMAP phase description and success criteria.
289	
290	</specifics>
291	
292	<deferred>
293	## Deferred Ideas
294	
295	None — discuss phase skipped.
296	
297	</deferred>
298	```
299	
300	Commit the minimal context:
301	
302	```bash
303	gsd_run query commit "docs(${PADDED_PHASE}): auto-generated context (discuss skipped)" --files "${phase_dir}/${padded_phase}-CONTEXT.md"
304	```
305	
306	Proceed to 3b.
307	
308	**If SKIP_DISCUSS is `false` (or unset):**
309	
310	**IMPORTANT — Discuss must be single-pass in autonomous mode.**
311	The discuss step in `--auto` mode MUST NOT loop. If CONTEXT.md already exists after discuss completes, do NOT re-invoke discuss for the same phase. The `has_context` check below is authoritative — once true, discuss is done for this phase regardless of perceived "gaps" in the context file.
312	
313	**If `INTERACTIVE` is set:** Run the standard discuss-phase skill inline (asks interactive questions, waits for user answers). This preserves user input on all design decisions while keeping plan+execute out of the main context:
314	
315	```
316	Skill(skill="gsd-discuss-phase", args="${PHASE_NUM}")
317	```
318	
319	**If `INTERACTIVE` is NOT set:** Execute the smart_discuss step for this phase (batch table proposals, auto-optimized).
320	
321	After discuss completes (either mode), verify context was written:
322	
323	```bash
324	PHASE_STATE=$(gsd_run query init.phase-op ${PHASE_NUM})
325	```
326	
327	Check `has_context`. If false → go to handle_blocker: "Discuss for phase ${PHASE_NUM} did not produce CONTEXT.md."
328	
329	**3a.5. UI Design Contract (Frontend Phases)**
330	
331	Resolve active `plan:pre` hooks:
332	
333	```bash
334	UI_SPEC_FILE=$(ls "${PHASE_DIR}"/*-UI-SPEC.md 2>/dev/null | head -1)
335	HOOKS_JSON=$(gsd_run loop render-hooks plan:pre --raw)
336	```
337	
338	Read the `activeHooks` array directly from `HOOKS_JSON` (in-context — do NOT invoke a shell pipeline). **Compute the active UI step hooks** = entries from `activeHooks` where `kind == "step"` and `ref.skill` is set. **If there are NO active step hooks → skip silently to 3b.** (This covers `workflow.ui_phase=false` — including configurations where only a gate-only entry is present, e.g. `ui_phase=false` + `ui_safety_gate=true` produces `activeHooks=[{kind:"gate"}]`. Autonomous never runs the plan:pre gate — it is always pipeline mode — so a gate-only active set is equivalent to no active step and is silently skipped here. This matches OLD §3a.5 behaviour.)
339	
340	(At least one active step hook ⇒ `workflow.ui_phase` is on.) Run the UI-SPEC gate:
341	
342	```bash
343	GATE=$(gsd_run check ui-plan-gate "${PHASE_NUM}" --raw)
344	```
345	
346	Read `frontend` and `hasUiSpec` from `GATE` (in-context).
347	
348	**If `frontend` is false:** Skip silently to 3b.
349	
350	**If `hasUiSpec` is true (UI-SPEC already exists):** Skip silently to 3b.
351	
352	**Otherwise (frontend phase + no UI-SPEC):** For each active step hook (the `kind == "step"` set from above, in array order):
353	
354	```
355	Skill(skill="gsd-${ref.skill}", args="${PHASE_NUM}")
356	```
357	
358	(Prepend `gsd-` to `ref.skill` — so `ui-phase` → `gsd-ui-phase`. Bare `${PHASE_NUM}` args — autonomous style, same pattern as the verify:post dispatch.) Entries where `kind == "gate"` are silently ignored — autonomous is always pipeline mode, there is no blocking gate here.
359	
360	After all step hooks return, re-read:
361	
362	```bash
363	UI_SPEC_FILE=$(ls "${PHASE_DIR}"/*-UI-SPEC.md 2>/dev/null | head -1)
364	```
365	
366	**If `UI_SPEC_FILE` is still empty:** Display warning `Phase ${PHASE_NUM}: UI-SPEC generation did not produce output — continuing without design contract.` and proceed to 3b. NON-BLOCKING.
367	
368	**3b. Plan**
369	
370	**If `INTERACTIVE` is set:** Background dispatch is only safe on a runtime where a backgrounded agent can still nest the pipeline's subagents (plan-checker / worktree executors / verifier). This is determined from the documentation-sourced dispatch capability in the registry (#1708); Claude Code's backgrounded agents have no `Agent`/`Task` tool, and every other runtime either prohibits nested subagents or disables them by default. So run **inline** everywhere except where `dispatch-should-flatten` returns `false`. Resolve first:
371	
372	```bash
373	FLATTEN=$(gsd_run query dispatch-should-flatten --raw 2>/dev/null || echo "true")
374	```
375	
376	- **If `FLATTEN` is `false`:** Dispatch plan as a background agent to keep the main context lean. While plan runs, the workflow can immediately start discussing the next phase (see step 4).
377	
378	  - If `PLAN_STRATEGY=converge`, print: `◆ Spawning background plan-convergence loop for phase ${PHASE_NUM}... (runs in a subagent — no output until it returns, ~1–5 min; expected, not a freeze)`
379	
380	  ```
381	  Agent(
382	    description="Plan convergence phase ${PHASE_NUM}: ${PHASE_NAME}",
383	    run_in_background=true,
384	    prompt="Run plan convergence for phase ${PHASE_NUM}: Skill(skill=\"gsd-plan-review-convergence\", args=\"${PHASE_NUM} ${CONVERGENCE_ARGS}\")"
385	  )
386	  ```
387	
388	  - Otherwise, print: `◆ Spawning background planner for phase ${PHASE_NUM}... (runs in a subagent — no output until it returns, ~1–5 min; expected, not a freeze)`
389	
390	  ```
391	  Agent(
392	    description="Plan phase ${PHASE_NUM}: ${PHASE_NAME}",
393	    run_in_background=true,
394	    prompt="Run plan-phase for phase ${PHASE_NUM}: Skill(skill=\"gsd-plan-phase\", args=\"${PHASE_NUM}\")"
395	  )
396	  ```
397	
398	  Store the agent task_id. After discuss for the next phase completes (or if no next phase), wait for the plan agent to finish before proceeding to execute.
399	
400	- **Otherwise (`FLATTEN` is `true` — run inline):** Run plan **inline** (do NOT background) so the plan-checker runs. The next phase's discuss does not overlap planning here — correctness over overlap.
401	
402	  - If `PLAN_STRATEGY=converge`:
403	
404	  ```
405	  Skill(skill="gsd-plan-review-convergence", args="${PHASE_NUM} ${CONVERGENCE_ARGS}")
406	  ```
407	
408	  - Otherwise (local planning):
409	
410	  ```
411	  Skill(skill="gsd-plan-phase", args="${PHASE_NUM}")
412	  ```
413	
414	**If `INTERACTIVE` is NOT set (default):** Run plan inline.
415	
416	If `PLAN_STRATEGY=converge`, run the convergence loop:
417	
418	```
419	Skill(skill="gsd-plan-review-convergence", args="${PHASE_NUM} ${CONVERGENCE_ARGS}")
420	```
421	
422	If `PLAN_STRATEGY=local`, run the regular planner:
423	
424	```
425	Skill(skill="gsd-plan-phase", args="${PHASE_NUM}")
426	```
427	
428	Verify plan produced output — re-run `init phase-op` and check `has_plans`. If false → go to handle_blocker: "Plan phase ${PHASE_NUM} did not produce any plans."
429	
430	**3c. Execute**
431	
432	**If `INTERACTIVE` is set:** Wait for the plan agent to complete (if not already) and verify plans exist. Background dispatch is only safe on a runtime where a backgrounded agent can still nest the pipeline's subagents (plan-checker / worktree executors / verifier). This is determined from the documentation-sourced dispatch capability in the registry (#1708); Claude Code's backgrounded agents have no `Agent`/`Task` tool, and every other runtime either prohibits nested subagents or disables them by default. So run **inline** everywhere except where `dispatch-should-flatten` returns `false`. Resolve first:
433	
434	```bash
435	FLATTEN=$(gsd_run query dispatch-should-flatten --raw 2>/dev/null || echo "true")
436	```
437	
438	- **If `FLATTEN` is `false`:** Dispatch execute as a background agent:
439	
440	```
441	Agent(
442	  description="Execute phase ${PHASE_NUM}: ${PHASE_NAME}",
443	  run_in_background=true,
444	  prompt="Run execute-phase for phase ${PHASE_NUM}: Skill(skill=\"gsd-execute-phase\", args=\"${PHASE_NUM} --no-transition\")"
445	)
446	```
447	
448	  Store the agent task_id. The workflow can now start discussing the next phase while this phase executes in the background. Before starting post-execution routing for this phase, wait for the execute agent to complete.
449	
450	- **Otherwise (`FLATTEN` is `true` — run inline):** Run execute **inline** (do NOT background) so worktree isolation and verification run:
451	
452	```
453	Skill(skill="gsd-execute-phase", args="${PHASE_NUM} --no-transition")
454	```
455	
456	**If `INTERACTIVE` is NOT set (default):** Run execute inline as before.
457	
458	```
459	Skill(skill="gsd-execute-phase", args="${PHASE_NUM} --no-transition")
460	```
461	
462	**3c.5. Code Review and Fix**
463	
464	Auto-invoke code review and fix chain. Autonomous mode chains both review and fix (unlike execute-phase/quick which only suggest fix).
465	
466	**Capability dispatch:**
467	```bash
468	EXECUTE_POST_HOOKS_JSON=$(gsd_run loop render-hooks execute:post --raw)
469	```
470	
471	Resolve active step hooks from `EXECUTE_POST_HOOKS_JSON` where `kind == "step"` and `ref.skill == "code-review"`.
472	
473	If no active code-review step hook exists: display "Code review skipped (code-review capability inactive)" and proceed to 3d. This covers `workflow.code_review=false` through the Capability Registry; do not query the code-review toggle directly here.
474	
475	For each active code-review step hook, dispatch the skill using the registry-provided stem:
476	
477	```
478	Skill(skill="gsd-${ref.skill}", args="${PHASE_NUM}")
479	```
480	
481	Parse status from REVIEW.md frontmatter. If "clean" or "skipped": proceed to 3d. If findings found after the capability-dispatched review, auto-invoke the consolidated fix entry point:
482	```
483	Skill(skill="gsd-code-review", args="${PHASE_NUM} --fix --auto")
484	```
485	
486	**Error handling:** If either Skill fails, catch the error, display as non-blocking, and proceed to 3d.
487	
488	**3d. Post-Execution Routing**
489	
490	After execute, read canonical verification:
491	
492	```bash
493	VERIFY_STATUS=$(gsd_run query verification.status "${PHASE_DIR}" 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.status//empty')
494	```
495	
496	If `PHASE_DIR` is absent, re-fetch `init.phase-op ${PHASE_NUM}` and parse `phase_dir`.
497	
498	If `VERIFY_STATUS` is empty, handle_blocker: "No verification results for phase ${PHASE_NUM}."
499	
500	**If `passed`:**
501	
502	Display `Phase ${PHASE_NUM} ✅ ${PHASE_NAME} — Verification passed`, run `@$HOME/.claude/gsd-core/workflows/transition.md`, then Proceed to iterate step.
503	
504	**If `stale`:** handle_blocker: "Stale verification for phase ${PHASE_NUM}."
505	
506	**If `human_needed`:**
507	
508	Read `human_verification` items. In text mode (`--text` or init `text_mode=true`), replace AskUserQuestion with a plain-text numbered list. Otherwise ask whether to validate now or continue without validation. If validating now, present items, then ask `Validation result?` with `All good — continue` / `Found issues`.
509	
510	On "All good — continue": set VERIFICATION frontmatter `status: passed`, display `Phase ${PHASE_NUM} ✅ Human validation passed`, run `@$HOME/.claude/gsd-core/workflows/transition.md`, then iterate.
511	
512	On "Found issues": Go to handle_blocker with the user's reported issues as the description.
513	
514	On **"Continue without validation"**: record an explicit deferred state and stop autonomous mode:
515	
516	```markdown
517	## Deferred Verification
518	
519	| Phase | State | Resume |
520	|-------|-------|--------|
521	| ${PHASE_NUM} | verification_deferred_human | /gsd-verify-work ${PHASE_NUM} |
522	```
523	
524	Append/update this STATE.md section, display `Phase ${PHASE_NUM} ⏭ verification_deferred_human — resume with /gsd-verify-work ${PHASE_NUM}`, then handle_blocker: "Human verification deferred for phase ${PHASE_NUM}."
525	
526	**If `gaps_found`:**
527	
528	Read gap score/items from VERIFICATION.md. Display:
529	```
530	⚠ Phase ${PHASE_NUM}: ${PHASE_NAME} — Gaps Found
531	Score: {N}/{M} must-haves verified
532	```
533	
534	Ask how to proceed: `Run gap closure` / `Continue without fixing` / `Stop autonomous mode`.
535	
536	On **"Run gap closure"**: one gap-closure attempt:
537	
538	```
539	Skill(skill="gsd-plan-phase", args="${PHASE_NUM} --gaps")
540	```
541	
542	Re-run `init phase-op ${PHASE_NUM}`; if `has_plans` is false, handle_blocker: "Gap closure planning for phase ${PHASE_NUM} did not produce plans."
543	
544	Re-execute:
545	```
546	Skill(skill="gsd-execute-phase", args="${PHASE_NUM} --no-transition")
547	```
548	
549	Re-read verification status:
550	```bash
551	VERIFY_STATUS=$(gsd_run query verification.status "${PHASE_DIR}" 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.status//empty')
552	```
553	
554	If `passed` or `human_needed`: route normally.
555	
556	If `stale`: handle_blocker: "Stale verification for phase ${PHASE_NUM}."
557	
558	If still `gaps_found` after this retry, display `Gaps persist after closure attempt.` and ask `Continue anyway` / `Stop autonomous mode`.
559	
560	On "Continue anyway": record `verification_deferred_gaps` using the table below, display `Phase ${PHASE_NUM} ⏭ verification_deferred_gaps — resume with /gsd-plan-phase ${PHASE_NUM} --gaps`, then handle_blocker: "Verification gaps deferred for phase ${PHASE_NUM}."
561	On "Stop autonomous mode": Go to handle_blocker.
562	
563	This limits gap closure to 1 retry.
564	
565	On **"Continue without fixing"**: record an explicit deferred state and stop autonomous mode:
566	
567	```markdown
568	## Deferred Verification
569	
570	| Phase | State | Resume |
571	|-------|-------|--------|
572	| ${PHASE_NUM} | verification_deferred_gaps | /gsd-plan-phase ${PHASE_NUM} --gaps |
573	```
574	
575	Append/update this STATE.md section, display `Phase ${PHASE_NUM} ⏭ verification_deferred_gaps — resume with /gsd-plan-phase ${PHASE_NUM} --gaps`, then handle_blocker: "Verification gaps deferred for phase ${PHASE_NUM}."
576	
577	On **"Stop autonomous mode"**: Go to handle_blocker with "User stopped — gaps remain in phase ${PHASE_NUM}".
578	
579	**3d.5. UI Review (Frontend Phases)**
580	
581	> Run only after `passed` or human verification was updated to `passed`.
582	
583	Resolve the active post-verification hooks and the UI-SPEC gate:
584	
585	```bash
586	UI_SPEC_FILE=$(ls "${PHASE_DIR}"/*-UI-SPEC.md 2>/dev/null | head -1)
587	HOOKS_JSON=$(gsd_run loop render-hooks verify:post --raw)
588	```
589	
590	Read the `activeHooks` array directly from the `HOOKS_JSON` value already in context (do not invoke a shell `jq` pipeline — parse as the JSON object it is). **If `activeHooks` is empty or absent:** skip silently to the iterate step.
591	
592	For each entry in `activeHooks` in array order where `kind == "step"` and `ref.skill` is set:
593	
594	- **Honor `consumes`:** if the hook's `consumes` array includes `"UI-SPEC.md"` and `UI_SPEC_FILE` is empty (no `*-UI-SPEC.md` exists in `PHASE_DIR`) → skip that hook (`onError: skip`). Hooks that do not declare `"UI-SPEC.md"` in their `consumes` proceed normally regardless of `UI_SPEC_FILE`.
595	- Invoke:
596	
597	```
598	Skill(skill="gsd-${ref.skill}", args="${PHASE_NUM}")
599	```
600	
601	(i.e. prepend `gsd-` to `ref.skill` — so `ui-review` → `gsd-ui-review`.)
602	
603	Display the review result summary and score from UI-REVIEW.md if produced. Continue to iterate step regardless of result — hooks at this point are advisory, not blocking.
604	
605	</step>

609	## Smart Discuss
610	
611	> Full instructions are in `gsd-core/references/autonomous-smart-discuss.md`. Read that file now and follow it exactly.
612	
613	Smart discuss is an autonomous-optimized variant of `gsd-discuss-phase`. It proposes grey area answers in batch tables — the user accepts or overrides per area — and writes an identical CONTEXT.md to what discuss-phase produces.
614	
615	**Inputs:** `PHASE_NUM` from execute_phase.
616	
617	Read and execute: `$HOME/.claude/gsd-core/references/autonomous-smart-discuss.md`

623	## 4. Iterate
624	
625	**If `ONLY_PHASE` is set:** Do not iterate. Proceed directly to lifecycle step (which exits cleanly per single-phase mode).
626	
627	**If `TO_PHASE` is set and current phase number >= `TO_PHASE`:** The target phase has been reached. Do not iterate further. Display:
628	
629	```
630	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
631	 GSD ► AUTONOMOUS ▸ --to ${TO_PHASE} REACHED
632	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
633	
634	 Completed through phase ${TO_PHASE} as requested.
635	 Remaining phases were not executed.
636	
637	 Resume with: /gsd-autonomous --from ${next_incomplete_phase}
638	```
639	
640	Proceed to lifecycle step (partial completion skips audit/complete/cleanup). Exit cleanly.
641	
642	**Otherwise:** After each phase, re-read manager projection:
643	
644	```bash
645	INIT_MANAGER=$(gsd_run query init.manager)
646	if [[ "$INIT_MANAGER" == @file:* ]]; then INIT_MANAGER=$(cat "${INIT_MANAGER#@file:}"); fi
647	STATE_CONTENT=$(cat .planning/STATE.md 2>/dev/null || true)
648	```
649	
650	Re-filter incomplete phases using discover_phases logic: keep phases where `phase_complete !== true` or `verification_status !== "passed"`, drop deferred phases from the autonomous queue, re-apply `--from` / `--to`, then sort by number ascending.
651	
652	Read STATE.md fresh:
653	
654	```bash
655	cat .planning/STATE.md
656	```
657	
658	Check for blockers in the Blockers/Concerns section. If blockers are found, go to handle_blocker with the blocker description.
659	
660	If incomplete phases remain: proceed to next phase, loop back to execute_phase.
661	
662	If no runnable phases remain but deferred phases were skipped, display `Autonomous run stopped with deferred verification phases still pending. Resume them with the commands listed in Deferred Verification.` Proceed to lifecycle only if every non-deferred phase is complete; otherwise go to handle_blocker.
663	
664	**Interactive mode overlap:** When `INTERACTIVE` is set, Codex can overlap discuss for Phase N+1 with background plan+execute for Phase N. Other runtimes keep plan/execute inline, so phases stay sequential:
665	1. After discuss completes for Phase N, dispatch plan+execute as background agents
666	2. Immediately start discuss for Phase N+1 (the next incomplete phase) while Phase N builds
667	3. Before starting plan for Phase N+1, wait for Phase N's execute agent to complete and handle its post-execution routing (verification, gap closure, etc.)
668	
669	The main context only accumulates discuss conversations; background plan/execute work stays isolated in its agents.
670	
671	If all phases complete, proceed to lifecycle step.
672	
673	</step>

804	<step name="handle_blocker">
805	
806	## 6. Handle Blocker
807	
808	When any phase operation fails or a blocker is detected, present 3 options via AskUserQuestion:
809	
810	**Prompt:** "Phase {N} ({Name}) encountered an issue: {description}"
811	
812	**Options:**
813	1. **"Fix and retry"** — Re-run the failed step (discuss, plan, or execute) for this phase
814	2. **"Skip this phase"** — Mark phase as skipped, continue to the next incomplete phase
815	3. **"Stop autonomous mode"** — Display summary of progress so far and exit cleanly
816	
817	**On "Fix and retry":** Loop back to the failed step within execute_phase. If the same step fails again after retry, re-present these options.
818	
819	**On "Skip this phase":** Log `Phase {N} ⏭ {Name} — Skipped by user` and proceed to iterate.
820	
821	**On "Stop autonomous mode":** Display progress summary:
822	
823	```
824	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
825	 GSD ► AUTONOMOUS ▸ STOPPED
826	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
827	
828	 Completed: {list of completed phases}
829	 Skipped: {list of skipped phases}
830	 Remaining: {list of remaining phases}
831	
832	 Resume with: /gsd-autonomous ${ONLY_PHASE ? "--only " + ONLY_PHASE : "--from " + next_phase}${TO_PHASE ? " --to " + TO_PHASE : ""}
833	```
834	
835	</step>
836	
837	</process>
838	
839	<success_criteria>
840	- [ ] All incomplete phases executed in order (smart discuss → ui-phase → plan → execute → ui-review each)
841	- [ ] Smart discuss proposes grey area answers in tables, user accepts or overrides per area
842	- [ ] Progress banners displayed between phases
843	- [ ] Execute-phase invoked with --no-transition (autonomous manages transitions)
844	- [ ] Post-execution verification reads VERIFICATION.md and routes on status
845	- [ ] Passed verification → automatic continue to next phase
846	- [ ] Human-needed verification → user prompted to validate or skip
847	- [ ] Gaps-found → user offered gap closure, continue, or stop
848	- [ ] Gap closure limited to 1 retry (prevents infinite loops)
849	- [ ] Plan-phase and execute-phase failures route to handle_blocker
850	- [ ] ROADMAP.md re-read after each phase (catches inserted phases)
851	- [ ] STATE.md checked for blockers before each phase
852	- [ ] Blockers handled via user choice (retry / skip / stop)
853	- [ ] Final completion or stop summary displayed
854	- [ ] After all phases complete, lifecycle step is invoked (not manual suggestion)
855	- [ ] Lifecycle transition banner displayed before audit
856	- [ ] Audit invoked via Skill(skill="gsd-audit-milestone")
857	- [ ] Audit result routing: passed → auto-continue, gaps_found → user decides, tech_debt → user decides
858	- [ ] Audit technical failure (no file/no status) routes to handle_blocker
859	- [ ] Complete-milestone invoked via Skill() with ${milestone_version} arg
860	- [ ] Cleanup invoked via Skill() — internal confirmation is acceptable (CTRL-01)
861	- [ ] Final completion banner displayed after lifecycle
862	- [ ] Progress bar uses phase number / total milestone phases (not position among incomplete), with fallback display when phase numbers exceed total
863	- [ ] Smart discuss documents relationship to discuss-phase with CTRL-03 note
864	- [ ] Frontend phases get UI-SPEC generated before planning (step 3a.5) if not already present
865	- [ ] Frontend phases get UI review audit after successful execution (step 3d.5) if UI-SPEC exists
866	- [ ] UI phase and UI review respect workflow.ui_phase and workflow.ui_review config toggles
867	- [ ] UI review is advisory (non-blocking) — phase proceeds to iterate regardless of score
868	- [ ] `--only N` restricts execution to exactly one phase
869	- [ ] `--only N` skips lifecycle step (audit/complete/cleanup)
870	- [ ] `--only N` exits cleanly after single phase completes
871	- [ ] `--only N` on already-complete phase exits with message
872	- [ ] `--only N` handle_blocker resume message uses --only flag
873	- [ ] `--to N` stops execution after phase N completes (halts at iterate step)
874	- [ ] `--to N` filters out phases with number > N during discovery
875	- [ ] `--to N` displays "Stopping after phase N" in startup banner
876	- [ ] `--to N` on already completed target exits with "already completed" message
877	- [ ] `--to N` compatible with `--from N` (run phases from M to N)
878	- [ ] `--to N` handle_blocker resume message preserves --to flag
879	- [ ] `--to N` skips lifecycle when not all milestone phases complete
880	- [ ] `--interactive` runs discuss inline via gsd-discuss-phase (asks questions, waits for user)
881	- [ ] `--interactive` dispatches plan and execute as background agents on Codex (the only runtime where a backgrounded agent can nest subagents); runs them inline on all other runtimes
882	- [ ] `--interactive` enables pipeline parallelism (discuss Phase N+1 while Phase N builds) on Codex; phases run sequentially on all other runtimes
883	- [ ] `--interactive` main context only accumulates discuss conversations on Codex (on all other runtimes, inline plan/execute also accumulate)
884	- [ ] `--interactive` waits for background agents before post-execution routing
885	- [ ] `--interactive` compatible with `--only`, `--from`, and `--to` flags
886	- [ ] `--converge` routes planning through `gsd-plan-review-convergence`
887	- [ ] `--cross-ai` is accepted as an alias for `--converge`
888	- [ ] `--converge` fails fast with enable instructions when `workflow.plan_review_convergence=false`
889	- [ ] `--converge` forwards reviewer selector flags and `--max-cycles N`
890	- [ ] Default autonomous planning remains `gsd-plan-phase` when convergence is not requested
891	</success_criteria>
892

1	<ui_patterns>
2	
3	Visual patterns for user-facing GSD output. Orchestrators @-reference this file.
4	
5	## Stage Banners
6	
7	Use for major workflow transitions.
8	
9	```
10	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
11	 GSD ► {STAGE NAME}
12	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
13	```
14	
15	**Stage names (uppercase):**
16	- `QUESTIONING`
17	- `RESEARCHING`
18	- `DEFINING REQUIREMENTS`
19	- `CREATING ROADMAP`
20	- `PLANNING PHASE {N}`
21	- `EXECUTING WAVE {N}`
22	- `VERIFYING`
23	- `PHASE {N} COMPLETE ✓`
24	- `MILESTONE COMPLETE 🎉`
25	
26	---
27	
28	## Checkpoint Boxes
29	
30	User action required. 62-character width.
31	
32	```
33	╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
34	║  CHECKPOINT: {Type}                                          ║
35	╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
36	
37	{Content}
38	
39	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
40	→ {ACTION PROMPT}
41	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42	```
43	
44	**Types:**
45	- `CHECKPOINT: Verification Required` → `→ Type "approved" or describe issues`
46	- `CHECKPOINT: Decision Required` → `→ Select: option-a / option-b`
47	- `CHECKPOINT: Action Required` → `→ Type "done" when complete`
48	
49	---
50	
51	## Status Symbols
52	
53	```
54	✓  Complete / Passed / Verified
55	✗  Failed / Missing / Blocked
56	◆  In Progress
57	○  Pending
58	⚡ Auto-approved
59	⚠  Warning
60	🎉 Milestone complete (only in banner)
61	```
62	
63	---
64	
65	## Progress Display
66	
67	**Phase/milestone level:**
68	```
69	Progress: ████████░░ 80%
70	```
71	
72	**Task level:**
73	```
74	Tasks: 2/4 complete
75	```
76	
77	**Plan level:**
78	```
79	Plans: 3/5 complete
80	```
81	
82	---
83	
84	## Spawning Indicators
85	
86	**Liveness convention:** Every spawn announcement must carry the canonical phrase `runs in a subagent` inline so users know that silence during a subagent run is expected. Without this, a healthy 1–5 minute agent looks identical to a frozen session. Single spawns use the singular form; parallel spawns use the plural form.
87	
88	```
89	◆ Spawning researcher... (runs in a subagent — no output until it returns, ~1–5 min; expected, not a freeze)
90	
91	◆ Spawning 4 researchers in parallel... (each runs in a subagent — no output until they return, ~1–5 min; expected, not a freeze)
92	  → Stack research
93	  → Features research
94	  → Architecture research
95	  → Pitfalls research
96	
97	✓ Researcher complete: STACK.md written
98	```
99	
100	---
101	
102	## Next Up Block
103	
104	Always at end of major completions.
105	
106	```
107	───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
108	
109	## ▶ Next Up
110	
111	**{Identifier}: {Name}** — {one-line description}
112	
113	`/clear` then:
114	
115	`{copy-paste command}`
116	
117	───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
118	
119	**Also available:**
120	- `/gsd-alternative-1` — description
121	- `/gsd-alternative-2` — description
122	
123	───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
124	```
125	
126	---
127	
128	## Error Box
129	
130	```
131	╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
132	║  ERROR                                                       ║
133	╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
134	
135	{Error description}
136	
137	**To fix:** {Resolution steps}
138	```
139	
140	---
141	
142	## Tables
143	
144	```
145	| Phase | Status | Plans | Progress |
146	|-------|--------|-------|----------|
147	| 1     | ✓      | 3/3   | 100%     |
148	| 2     | ◆      | 1/4   | 25%      |
149	| 3     | ○      | 0/2   | 0%       |
150	```
151	
152	---
153	
154	## Anti-Patterns
155	
156	- Varying box/banner widths
157	- Mixing banner styles (`===`, `---`, `***`)
158	- Skipping `GSD ►` prefix in banners
159	- Random emoji (`🚀`, `✨`, `💫`)
160	- Missing Next Up block after completions
161	
162	</ui_patterns>
163

planning via /gsd-plan-phase --reviews.

**Flow:** Detect CLIs → Build review prompt → Invoke each CLI → Collect responses → Write REVIEWS.md

- `--gemini` — Include Gemini CLI review
- `--claude` — Include Claude CLI review (uses separate session)
- `--codex` — Include Codex CLI review
- `--opencode` — Include OpenCode review (uses model from user's OpenCode config)
- `--qwen` — Include Qwen Code review (Alibaba Qwen models)

- `--agy` / `--antigravity` — Include Antigravity CLI review

reviewers that are detected; otherwise review with all available CLIs. Configured
`review.reviewer_instances` names may appear in `review.default_reviewers`; each runs as an
independent reviewer identity backed by its configured adapter+model (see

gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR:-${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/opencode}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR:-${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/opencode}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; elif [ -f "${KILO_CONFIG_DIR:-${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/kilo}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}" ]; then GSD_TOOLS="${KILO_CONFIG_DIR:-${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/kilo}/gsd-core/bin/${_GSD_SHIM_NAME}"; gsd_run() { node "$GSD_TOOLS" "$@"; }; else echo "ERROR: gsd-tools.cjs not found at $GSD_TOOLS and gsd-tools is not on PATH. Run: npx -y @opengsd/gsd-core@latest --claude --local" >&2; exit 1; fi; if [ -n "${CLAUDE_ENV_FILE:-}" ] && [ -n "${GSD_TOOLS:-}" ]; then printf "export PATH='%s':\"\$PATH\"\n" "${GSD_TOOLS%/*}" >> "$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true; fi
18	# Check each CLI
19	command -v gemini >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "gemini:available" || echo "gemini:missing"
20	command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "claude:available" || echo "claude:missing"
21	command -v codex >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "codex:available" || echo "codex:missing"
22	command -v coderabbit >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "coderabbit:available" || echo "coderabbit:missing"
23	command -v opencode >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "opencode:available" || echo "opencode:missing"
24	command -v qwen >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "qwen:available" || echo "qwen:missing"
25	command -v cursor-agent >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "cursor:available" || echo "cursor:missing"
26	command -v agy >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "antigravity:available" || echo "antigravity:missing"
27	
28	# Check local model servers (OpenAI-compatible HTTP API — no CLI binary required)
29	OLLAMA_HOST=$(gsd_run query config-get review.ollama_host 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
30	if [ -z "$OLLAMA_HOST" ] || [ "$OLLAMA_HOST" = "null" ]; then OLLAMA_HOST="http://localhost:11434"; fi
31	curl -s --max-time 2 "${OLLAMA_HOST}/v1/models" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "ollama:available" || echo "ollama:missing"
32	
33	LM_STUDIO_HOST=$(gsd_run query config-get review.lm_studio_host 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
34	if [ -z "$LM_STUDIO_HOST" ] || [ "$LM_STUDIO_HOST" = "null" ]; then LM_STUDIO_HOST="http://localhost:1234"; fi
35	curl -s --max-time 2 "${LM_STUDIO_HOST}/v1/models" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "lm_studio:available" || echo "lm_studio:missing"
36	
37	LLAMA_CPP_HOST=$(gsd_run query config-get review.llama_cpp_host 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
38	if [ -z "$LLAMA_CPP_HOST" ] || [ "$LLAMA_CPP_HOST" = "null" ]; then LLAMA_CPP_HOST="http://localhost:8080"; fi
39	curl -s --max-time 2 "${LLAMA_CPP_HOST}/v1/models" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "llama_cpp:available" || echo "llama_cpp:missing"
